en.extract:
The Banda myzomela (Myzomela boiei) is a bird species in the family Meliphagidae. It is endemic to Indonesia, and found in the Banda, Babar and the Tanimbar Islands. The two subspecies usually recognised are separated by 350 km (220 mi) of open sea. 


Taxonomy
The Banda myzomela was formally described in 1843 by the German naturalist Salomon Müller as Nectarinia (Myzomela) Boiei based on specimens collected on the Banda Islands. The specific epithet boiei was chosen to honour the memory of the German zoologist Heinrich Boie.
Two subspecies are recognised:

M. b. boiei (Müller, S, 1843) – found on the Banda Islands (eastern Lesser Sunda Islands)
M. b. annabellae Sclater, PL, 1883 – found on the islands of Yamdena and Selaru, Tanimbar Islands (eastern Lesser Sunda Islands)

In 2025, a study proposed that the two subspecies could be raised to the species level because of notable differences in plumage, size and song. The subspecies M. b. annabellae would be elevated to full species (Tanimbar myzomela M. annabellae), with the population on Babar also split as a new species, Babar myzomela Myzomela babarensis.


Description
The species has sexual dimorphism. The male has a distinctive red head, neck, upper breast, and mantle, black eye ring, scapulars, and wings, a distinctive pointed beak and brown legs. The female is primarily brown, with an orange part underneath the also-pointed beak. The juveniles are not described. The subspecies M. b. annabellae differs from subspecies M. b. boiei in that the males have a white chest instead of a black one. 


Habitat
Its natural habitats are subtropical or tropical moist lowland forests, subtropical or tropical mangrove forests, and subtropical or tropical moist montane forests. It is a possible nomadic species that changes its living place in the region and go foraging in twos (probably pairs), and sometimes in flocks. They have their eggs in September and the chicks fledge at April.


References

*Dark grey: Banda myzomela ''Myzomela boiei'' *Pink: Tanimbar myzomela ''Myzomela annabellae'' *Light blue: Babar myzomela ''Myzomela babarensis'' | synonyms = }} The '''Banda myzomela''' ('''''Myzomela boiei''''') is a [[bird]] species in the family [[Meliphagidae]]. It is [[Endemism|endemic]] to [[Indonesia]], and found in the [[Banda Island|Banda]], [[Babar Island|Babar]] and the [[Tanimbar Islands]]. The two [[subspecies]] usually recognised are separated by {{cvt|350|km}} of open sea. == Taxonomy == The Banda myzomela was [[species description|formally described]] in 1843 by the German naturalist [[Salomon Müller]] as ''Nectarinia'' (''Myzomela'') ''Boiei'' based on specimens collected on the [[Banda Islands]].{{ cite book | last=Müller | first=Salomon | author-link=Salomon Müller | date=1843 | title=Verhandelingen over de natuurlijke geschiedenis | volume=Land-en Volkenkunde | language=Dutch | location=Leiden | publisher=In commissie bij. S. en J. Luchtmans en C.C. van der Hoek | page=172, Footnote | url=https://www.biodiversitylibrary.org/page/56902010 }} Published in parts. For the publication date see: {{ cite journal | last1=Husson | first1=A.M. | last2=Holthuis | first2=L.B. | date=1955 | title=The dates of publication of "Verhandelingen over de natuurlijke Geschiedenis der Nederlandsche overzeesche Bezittingen" edited by C. J. Temminck | journal=Zoologische Mededelingen | volume=34 | issue=2 | pages=17-24 | url=https://repository.naturalis.nl/pub/318953 }} The earliest description cited above was included in Part 6 of the volume ''Land-en Volkenkunde'' that was published in June 1843. An illustration of the Banda myzomela was published in March 1844 as [https://www.biodiversitylibrary.org/page/51851508 Plate 10] in part 10 of the ''Zoologie'' volume. The text on the Banda myzomela in the ''Zoologie'' volume on [https://www.biodiversitylibrary.org/page/51851485 page 66] was published in part 12 in June 1845.{{ cite book | editor-last=Paynter | editor-first=Raymond A. Jr | year=1986 | title=Check-list of Birds of the World | volume=12 | publisher=Museum of Comparative Zoology | place=Cambridge, Massachusetts | page=358 | url=https://www.biodiversitylibrary.org/page/14482475 }} The specific epithet ''boiei'' was chosen to honour the memory of the German zoologist [[Heinrich Boie]].{{ cite web | last=Jobling | first=James A. | title=Boiei | work=The Key to Scientific Names | url=https://birdsoftheworld.org/bow/key-to-scientific-names/search?q=Boiei | publisher=Cornell Lab of Ornithology | access-date=7 May 2026}} Two subspecies are recognised:{{ cite web | author=AviList Core Team | date=2025 | title=AviList: The Global Avian Checklist, v2025 | doi=10.2173/avilist.v2025 | doi-access=free | url=http://www.avilist.org/checklist/v2025/ | access-date=7 May 2026 }} * ''M. b. boiei'' ([[Salomon Müller|Müller, S]], 1843) – found on the [[Banda Islands]] (eastern [[Lesser Sunda Islands]]) * ''M. b. annabellae'' [[Philip Sclater|Sclater, PL]], 1883 – found on the islands of [[Yamdena]] and [[Selaru]], [[Tanimbar Islands]] (eastern [[Lesser Sunda Islands]]) [[File:Myzomela babarensis, Babar Island, Maluku, Indonesia 433880425 (cropped).jpg|left|thumb|Myzomela on Babar Island, Maluku, Indonesia (''M. babarensis'', formerly ''M. boiei annabellae'' pro parte)]] In 2025, a study proposed that the two subspecies could be raised to the species level because of notable differences in plumage, size and song. The subspecies ''M. b. annabellae'' would be elevated to full species ([[Tanimbar myzomela]] ''M. annabellae''), with the population on Babar also split as a new species, Babar myzomela ''Myzomela babarensis''.{{cite journal |last1=Berryman|first1=Alex J. |last2=Spencer|first2=Andrew J. |last3=Sharma|first3=Puja |last4=Eaton |first4=James A.|year=2025|title=A taxonomic revision of Banda Myzomela ''Myzomela boiei'' (S. Müller 1843), including the description of a new species from Babar Island, Indonesia|journal=Bulletin of the British Ornithologists' Club|volume=145|issue=1|pages= 35–48|doi=10.25226/bboc.v145i1.2025.a4 |url=https://bioone.org/journals/bulletin-of-the-british-ornithologists-club/volume-145/issue-1/bboc.v145i1.2025.a4/A-taxonomic-revision-of-Banda-Myzomela-Myzomela-boiei-S-M%c3%bcller/10.25226/bboc.v145i1.2025.a4.full|doi-access=free}} == Description == The species has [[sexual dimorphism]]. The [[male]] has a distinctive red head, neck, upper breast, and mantle, black eye ring, scapulars, and wings, a distinctive pointed beak and brown legs. The [[female]] is primarily brown, with an orange part underneath the also-pointed beak. The [[Juvenile (organism)|juveniles]] are not described. The subspecies ''M. b. annabellae'' differs from subspecies ''M. b. boiei'' in that the males have a white chest instead of a black one. == Habitat == Its natural [[habitat]]s are subtropical or tropical moist lowland [[forest]]s, subtropical or tropical mangrove forests, and subtropical or tropical moist [[montane forest]]s. It is a possible [[nomadic]] [[species]] that changes its living place in the region and go foraging in twos (probably pairs), and sometimes in flocks. They have their eggs in September and the chicks fledge at April.{{Cite web |last=Higgins, Peter; Christidis, Les; Ford, Hugh. |first= |date=4 March 2020 |title=Banda Myzomela (Myzomela boiei) |url=https://birdsoftheworld.org/bow/species/banmyz1/cur/introduction |website=Banda Myzomela (Myzomela boiei)}} ==References== {{Reflist}} {{Taxonbar|from=Q1590848}} [[Category:Myzomela|Banda myzomela]] [[Category:Birds of Maluku]] [[Category:Birds of the Tanimbar Islands]] [[Category:Banda Islands]] [[Category:Birds described in 1843|Banda myzomela]] [[Category:Taxonomy articles created by Polbot]] [[Category:Taxa named by Salomon Müller]] {{Meliphagidae-stub}}
